body:
## Bug

The Rust CLI's `--capture browser` flag does not actually launch a headless browser. It fetches HTML via HTTP requests identically to `--capture api`.

## Steps to reproduce

Capture any Google Doc in archive mode with both methods:

```bash
# Rust v0.3.1
web-capture "https://docs.google.com/document/d/<DOC_ID>/edit" --capture api --archive -o doc-api.zip --verbose
web-capture "https://docs.google.com/document/d/<DOC_ID>/edit" --capture browser --archive -o doc-browser.zip --verbose
```

## Expected behavior

`--capture browser` should launch a headless browser (Chromium via `browser-commander`) and render the page, producing output that reflects browser-rendered content.

## Actual behavior

Both commands produce **byte-identical** output (same markdown size, same image count, same content). The only differences are Google's rotating `ust`/`usg` URL signature parameters in links, which change on every request regardless of method.

The verbose logs confirm no browser is launched — both show:
```
INFO web_capture::html: Fetching HTML from URL: ...
INFO web_capture::markdown: Converting HTML to Markdown
```

For Google Docs specifically, both modes route through `/export?format=html`.

## Tested with

- Rust `web-capture` v0.3.1
- Two different public Google Docs with tables, images, and links
- Archive format with markdown

## Test results

| Capture flag | Lines | Size | Images |
|-------------|-------|------|--------|
| `--capture api` | 302 | 19,331 bytes | 37 |
| `--capture browser` | 302 | 19,331 bytes | 37 |

Output is identical in both cases.

## Impact

The `--capture browser` flag is effectively a no-op in the Rust version. Users who explicitly request browser capture (e.g., for JavaScript-rendered pages or to get faithful Google Docs rendering per #72) silently get an HTTP fetch instead.
title: Rust: --capture browser does not launch a browser, produces identical output to --capture api
